growth_records.cite: Alpers et al. Replication-associated gene dosage effects shape the genomes of fast-growing bacteria but only for transcription and translation genes. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01130-22. [PMC10134858]
growth_records.snippet: P. aeruginosa cultures reached an OD600 of 1.8 ± 0.24 from a starting OD600 of 0.05 within 4 h and a maximum doubling time of 34 ± 1 min when grown under standard laboratory conditions
growth_records.notes: First (fast/exponential) growth phase, OD600-based doubling time = 34 ± 1 min = 0.5667 h (0-4h of a 10h LB batch culture at 37°C, 160 rpm). mu = ln2 / 0.5667 h = 1.223 h^-1.

growth_records.cite: Alpers et al. Replication-associated gene dosage effects shape the genomes of fast-growing bacteria but only for transcription and translation genes. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01130-22. [PMC10134858]
growth_records.snippet: Cell numbers, too, increased exponentially in the first 4 h from 4.5 × 10^7 to 7.3 × 10^8 ± 1.8 × 10^8 cells/mL with a doubling time of 30 ± 9 min
growth_records.notes: First (fast/exponential) growth phase, direct cell-count (viable/microscopic count) based doubling time = 30 ± 9 min = 0.5 h (0-4h of the same LB batch culture, 37°C, 160 rpm). mu = ln2 / 0.5 h = 1.386 h^-1.

Substrate uptake reported as %w/w oil consumed; NOT convertible to mmol/gDW/h because paper gives neither soybean-oil density nor time-resolved biomass, so absolute qS cannot be derived without an out-of-paper density assumption. DERIVED PHA titre: Pv*t = 0.0053 g/l/h * 144 h (6 d) = 0.763 g/l; cross-check PHA/CDM*CDM = 0.405*1.9 = 0.770 g/l (consistent) -> ~0.76 g/l. No growth rate/doubling time derivable: growth is explicitly non-linear (per-day increments differ) and only the day-6 maximum CDM is given, no exponential-phase OD slope. PHA is an intracellular storage polymer (accumulated, not truly secreted); listed under secretion_rates as the fermentation product.

growth_records.snippet: The growth rate at 23 °C of this P. aeruginosa strain in the minimal medium used was measured in batch culture to be 0.16 h−1. ... The minimal growth medium contained: 5 mmol l−1 glycerol, 0.9 mmol l−1 sodium glutamate, 145 mmol l−1 NaCl, 3
growth_records.notes: Only one directly MEASURED prokaryote growth-physiology datum in this reaction-diffusion modelling paper: mu_o=0.16 h-1 for P. aeruginosa PAO1/pAB1 in batch culture, 23 °C, in the stated defined minimal medium (the same medium as the flow-cell biofilm of Case 3). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.16 = 4.33 h (calculated). No uptake or secretion rates measured. Other growth rates in the paper (e.g. Case 2 P. aeruginosa single-cell rates ~0.025–1 h-1 in cystic-fibrosis sputum, and Case 4A K. pneumoniae biofilm rates 0.025–0.71 h-1) are NOT original measurements here: Case 2 rates are pooled single-cell measurements taken FROM Kragh et al. (ref 25), and the Case 4A K. pneumoniae rates are MODEL-PREDICTED (simulated), not measured — therefore not emitted as records. Iron identified as growth-limiting substrate (medium had no added trace elements, high phosphate → FePO4 precipitation).

growth_records.notes: Table 1 gives measured biofilm growth rate mu=0.300 h-1 for AUM (anaerobic, +50 mM nitrate, 37C), OD578 at stationary phase=0.53, time to stationary phase=24 h, viable cells=1.01x10^8/ml (mean of three independent experiments in duplicate). doubling_time computed td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.300=2.31 h. growth_records.cite: Syed Adnan Hasan et al. Degradation kinetics and pathway of phenol by Pseudomonas and Bacillus species. Biotechnology & Biotechnological Equipment (2015). DOI:10.1080/13102818.2014.991638. [PMC4684072]
growth_records.snippet: With strain IES-Ps-1, IES-S and IES-B, the maximum specific growth rates were 0.26, 0.43 and 0.78 h-1, respectively (Figure 1). However, specific growth rates started to decrease when phenol concentrations were above 220, 310 and 320 mg/L
growth_records.notes: Observed maximum specific growth rate mu=0.26 h-1 measured from OD600 slope in NB (grown aerobically 35C, 125 rpm, batch shake flask). Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.26=2.67 h. Growth inhibition onset above 220 mg/L phenol. Model-FITTED mu_max for IES-Ps-1 (Table 2): Monod 0.30, Luong-Levenspiel 0.38, Haldane-Andrew 0.60 h-1 (Ks Monod 98 mg/L, Luong 111, Haldane 234 mg/L; Ki(Haldane)=394 mg/L; Sm(Luong)=2000 mg/L, n=1) - regression fits, not direct measurements, so observed 0.26 h-1 reported. Phenol degraded to catechol then via ortho-cleavage (catechol 1,2-dioxygenase) to cis,cis-muconate. Phenol uptake not convertible to mmol/gDW/h: no Yxs or gDW biomass reported; tracked only as residual mg/L. Strain = Pseudomonas aeruginosa (originally isolated on malathion).

up.units: relative units (MFA normalization basis; absolute mmol/gDW/h not measured)

mfa.note: All PAO1 fluxes normalized to glucose uptake = 100; absolute rate not determined because tubular reactors contained both planktonic+biofilm cells
Dominant glucose catabolic route (PAO1 lacks phosphofructokinase; F6P->FBP deleted from model). ED-EMP (EDEMP) cycle active: G6P->6PG->GAP->F6P->G6P
Active in planktonic cells; very little malate dehydrogenase flux, significant OAA synthesized from pyruvate
Planktonic cells had moderately HIGHER TCA flux than biofilm cells

yields.note: No absolute biomass/product yields reported; 13C-MFA reports only relative fluxes normalized to glucose uptake=100

growth_records.cite: Wan, Ni et al. Bacterial Metabolism During Biofilm Growth Investigated by 13C Tracing. Frontiers in Microbiology (2018). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2018.02657. [PMC6255981]
growth_records.snippet: 13C-MFA profiled relative fluxes by assuming glucose uptake rate as 100 units ... Most flux values in biofilm cells differed within 20% compared to planktonic cells ... flux network showed a complete carbohydrate degradation loop: Entner-Do
growth_records.notes: 13C-MFA (WUflux) on proteinogenic amino acids from [1,2-13C]glucose. Fluxes are RELATIVE, normalized to glucose uptake = 100 (authors state absolute glucose utilization not measurable due to mixed planktonic+biofilm biomass). Individual numeric reaction fluxes are only in Figure 4 (image, 'best fit +/- CI', biological duplicates) and are NOT in text - not transcribed to avoid fabrication. Temperature = 'room temperature' (no numeric value). pH not stated. Biomass composition for MFA modified from Bartell et al. 2016. Dynamic 13C-pulse labeling: planktonic G6P labeling saturates within 5 min vs 180 min for biofilm (biofilm glucose metabolism much slower), free glutamate 20-25% labeled at 5 min in both modes.

growth_records.snippet: median growth rate of recipient without addition of pyochelin = 1.35 divisions per hour +/- 0.029 standard error
growth_records.notes: Sole carbon source = succinate (33.87 mM succinic acid). Reported max growth rate = 1.35 divisions per hour (SE 0.029), n=9. No substrate uptake or product secretion rates were measured in this paper (study focused on siderophore-mediated iron competition; only growth curves/OD, growth rate, lag, yield and flow-cytometry frequencies reported). growth_records.snippet: perfused with sterile LB at a dilution rate of 0.1 h-1. The chemostat was run over 48 h for the culture to achieve the steady state, with a constant population size of 3.6 x 10^7 cfu/ml being established
growth_records.notes: P. aeruginosa PAO1 grown in LB chemostat at imposed D=0.1/h, 37C, 300 rpm => steady-state mu=D=0.1/h (calculated). td=ln2/0.1=6.931 h. Steady-state density 3.6e7 cfu/mL.
